paragraphs: Diet, Religion, Allergy, and Fasting Constraint Specification
DOC-18 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope
Defines hard and soft constraints for vegetarian, non-vegetarian, eggetarian, Jain, vegan, allergies, fasting days, onion/garlic restrictions, and lifecycle-specific safety filters.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Constraint Hierarchy
5. Diet Modes
6. Fasting Logic

Fasting should be modeled as a mode/day/member-level override. A household may have one fasting member while others eat normal meals. Do not force the whole family into fasting meals unless the whole household selected it.
7. Implementation Order
Resolve household-wide hard constraints.
Resolve member-specific hard constraints.
Filter classes if the class itself is incompatible.
Filter dish candidates by ingredient and diet tags.
Filter add-on candidates by target member constraints.
Apply soft preference demotions after hard filters.
Log excluded items for explainability and QA.
8. Example

A Jain family with a toddler should receive Jain-compatible primary meal classes and Jain-compatible toddler add-ons. A non-Jain family with one fasting member should receive normal household meals plus fasting add-on/substitution for that member.
9. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-02, BUILD-04, BUILD-06.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
10. Acceptance Criteria
No hard constraint violation is possible in dish candidates or add-ons.
Fasting can be member-specific or household-wide.
Jain, vegan, allergy, and non-veg logic are explicit and testable.
11. Change Log
tables: Defines hard and soft constraints for vegetarian, non-vegetarian, eggetarian, Jain, vegan, allergies, fasting days, onion/garlic restrictions, and lifecycle-specific safety filters.
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Build dynamic onboarding, household/member capture, diet and city inputs, and profile object.
Generate 7-day meal-class plan from cohort matrix, regional rules, city overlay, weekday/weekend rhythm, variety rules.
Dish Expansion and Food DNA Ranking
Expand selected meal classes into dish candidate pools and rank dishes using Food DNA + constraints + context.
Exclude dish and add-on if ingredient or sub-ingredient contains allergen.
Exclude incompatible dishes/classes: veg, vegan, Jain, egg-only, non-veg.
Exclude onion, garlic, root vegetables, and non-Jain dishes.
Use fasting-compatible classes for active day/member.
Exclude unsafe foods for target member; household may still see normal primary meal.
Demote rather than exclude unless user marks Never.
Plant foods and dairy where allowed.
Meat, fish, seafood, egg unless separately allowed.
Dairy, ghee, butter, paneer, honey if configured.
Meat, fish, egg, onion, garlic, root vegetables.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
